summary: Sara Blakely is the self-made billionaire founder of Spanx and Sneex. This Forbes profile highlights her journey from a door-to-door fax salesperson with $5,000 in savings to a category-creating entrepreneur who relies on deep consumer empathy, intuition, and a commitment to elevating women.

principles.statement: Caring deeply can overcome a lack of resources or industry expertise.
principles.rationale: She built a billion-dollar brand without prior experience in the apparel industry or significant startup capital, succeeding simply because she was more invested in the problem than anyone else.
principles.source_quote: I did not have the most experience in the industry or the most money, but I cared the most.

principles.rationale: She views it as her personal calling to help other female founders and women succeed in business.

mental_models.name: Embodying the Consumer
mental_models.description: Putting yourself literally in the shoes of the consumer to understand their heart and mind, ensuring the product actually serves their real-world needs.
mental_models.example: Creating Spanx from pantyhose to wear under white slacks, and designing Sneex to make high-heeled sneakers comfortable.

mental_models.name: Intuition and Universal Signs
mental_models.description: Relying on intuition and actively asking the Universe for signs to guide major business decisions, rather than relying strictly on conventional business logic.
mental_models.example: Using her intuition to guide the $1.2 billion majority stake sale of Spanx to Blackstone.

heuristics: Carve out dedicated time for daydreaming and seeking ideas, even if it means just sitting parked in your driveway.

signature_quotes.text: I did not have the most experience in the industry or the most money, but I cared the most.
signature_quotes.context: Reflecting on her success with Spanx despite starting with only $5,000 and no industry background.

signature_quotes.text: I feel the divine feminine rising
signature_quotes.context: Discussing her intuition and the $1.2 billion Blackstone exit at the Forbes Power Women's summit.
